name: Dockerfile Excellence
description: Ten-gate Dockerfile enforcement: multi-stage architecture, layer optimization, portability, security, testing, CI, documentation, UX design, release readiness, truth. Hadolint zero warnings, pinned base images, non-root runtime, minimal attack surface, small image size, low memory, low CPU.

phases.name: Architecture & Idioms
phases.principle: Is the structure sound, modular, and idiomatic?

delegates.task_template: Enforce multi-stage build architecture for: ${goal}. Every Dockerfile MUST use multi-stage builds: strictly separate build-time dependencies (compilers, headers, dev tools, test frameworks) from the final runtime image. Final stage MUST use minimal base (alpine, distroless, or scratch where possible). Name every stage (FROM ... AS builder, FROM ... AS runtime). Verify: build artifacts COPY --from=builder, no compiler/header packages in final image, no package manager cache in final image. Enforce single responsibility: one process per container, no supervisord or multi-process patterns. Verify ENTRYPOINT + CMD separation (ENTRYPOINT for binary, CMD for default args). Flag: multiple FROM without named stages, COPY from unnamed stages, final image >100MB for compiled languages or >200MB for interpreted. Score Tech Debt (target 10/10) and Modularity (target 100/100).

Enforce Dockerfile best practices and idioms for: ${goal}. Flag: latest tag on base images (pin exact version: node:22.4-alpine3.20), RUN with apt-get install without --no-install-recommends, RUN apt-get install without && apt-get clean && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*, ADD where COPY suffices (ADD only for URLs or tar extraction), multiple RUN apt-get update (combine with install), COPY . . without .dockerignore, ENV for secrets (use --mount=type=secret), SHELL instruction without justification, MAINTAINER (use LABEL), using root user in final stage. Enforce: COPY --chown for file ownership, HEALTHCHECK instruction, LABEL for metadata (org.opencontainers.image.*), ARG for build-time variables with defaults, heredoc syntax for multi-line RUN (<<EOF), --mount=type=cache for package managers (apt, pip, npm).
verifies: base images pinned to exact versions (no latest)

delegates.task_template: Conduct deep research on current Dockerfile best practices for: ${goal}. Research: BuildKit features (cache mounts, secret mounts, SSH mounts, heredoc syntax), distroless vs Alpine vs scratch trade-offs, multi-platform builds (docker buildx, --platform), current OCI image spec and label standards, Dockerfile frontend syntax (# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1), Docker Scout and Snyk container scanning, Chainguard images as hardened alternatives, slim toolkit for image analysis, SBOM generation (syft, trivy). Cross-validate findings against Docker official docs, BuildKit docs, and OCI spec. Flag deprecated patterns (MAINTAINER, legacy builder features).
verifies: distroless vs Alpine vs scratch compared
OCI image spec and labels verified

delegates.task_template: Audit .dockerignore and build context for: ${goal}. Verify .dockerignore exists and excludes: .git, .gitignore, README*, LICENSE, docs/, tests/, node_modules/, __pycache__/, .env*, *.md, .vscode/, .idea/, .DS_Store, *.log, docker-compose*.yml (not needed in image), Dockerfile* (not needed in image), .dockerignore itself, large temp/data files. Verify build context size is minimal (docker build outputs context size). Verify Dockerfile naming: Dockerfile for single, Dockerfile.<variant> for multi (not dockerfile or DOCKERFILE). Verify docker-compose.yml (if present) references correct Dockerfiles. Remove orphaned Dockerfiles not referenced by any compose or CI config. Verify .env.example exists if .env is gitignored.
verifies: no large temp/data files in build context

phases.checkpoint: Multi-stage, pinned bases, clean context, modern idioms, best practices researched, files organized
phases.failure_message: Architecture gate failed. Use multi-stage builds, pin base images, and fix .dockerignore.

phases.name: Performance & Resources
phases.principle: Is every operation fast, lean, and resource-efficient?

delegates.task_template: Audit layer ordering and image size for: ${goal}. Enforce optimal layer caching: least-changing instructions first (FROM, ENV, ARG), dependency manifests next (COPY package.json, COPY requirements.txt), dependency install (RUN npm ci, RUN pip install), frequently changing files last (COPY . .). Combine RUN commands to minimize layer count (single RUN with && for related operations). Use --mount=type=cache for package managers: apt (/var/cache/apt), pip (/root/.cache/pip), npm (/root/.cache/npm), go (/go/pkg/mod). Verify: no unnecessary files in final image (use dive or docker history to inspect layers), no dev dependencies in production, no package manager cache in final layers. Measure image size: flag final images >100MB for Go/Rust/C, >200MB for Node/Python. Verify docker build time with warm cache <30s.
verifies: RUN commands combined for related operations
no dev dependencies in final image
no package manager cache in final layers

delegates.task_template: Audit base image health and runtime efficiency for: ${goal}. Verify base images: actively maintained (updated within 3 months), from official or verified publishers, minimal variant used (alpine over debian, slim over full). Flag images with known vulnerabilities (docker scout cves or trivy image). Verify: no unnecessary packages installed (curl/wget removed after use or multi-stage), no debug tools in production (bash, shell, strace), runtime user has minimal filesystem access. Check multi-platform support: verify base images support linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 at minimum. Verify HEALTHCHECK uses lightweight probe (not full application request). Verify resource limits documented (--memory, --cpus recommendations).
verifies: no known CVEs in base images (scout/trivy)
no debug tools in production image

phases.checkpoint: Layers optimized, image lean, base images verified, multi-platform ready
phases.failure_message: Performance gate failed. Poor layer ordering, bloated image, or vulnerable base detected.

phases.name: Portability & Platform Parity
phases.principle: Does it build and pass identically on every platform?

delegates.task_template: Verify cross-platform container portability for: ${goal}. Verify docker buildx build succeeds for: linux/amd64, linux/arm64 (Apple Silicon, AWS Graviton). Test on: Docker Desktop (macOS), Docker Engine (Linux), Podman (rootless). Verify no platform-specific assumptions: no hardcoded architecture paths (/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu), no architecture-specific binaries without multi-arch handling. Verify alpine-based images work without glibc (or use alpine with glibc compat). Test Dockerfile with both BuildKit (DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1) and legacy builder. Verify container runs identically on: Docker, Podman, containerd/nerdctl, Kubernetes (CRI-O). Verify timezone handling (TZ env var, no hardcoded /etc/localtime). Verify locale handling (C.UTF-8 or explicit LANG).
verifies: runs on Docker Desktop, Docker Engine, Podman
works with BuildKit and legacy builder

phases.checkpoint: Multi-arch builds verified, runtime parity on Docker/Podman/Kubernetes
phases.failure_message: Portability gate failed. Architecture-specific assumptions or runtime incompatibilities detected.

phases.name: Safety & Security
phases.principle: Is the code safe and the supply chain clean?

delegates.task_template: Security audit of all Dockerfiles for: ${goal}. Verify: final stage runs as non-root user (USER directive with numeric UID, not root). Flag: EXPOSE 22 (SSH in container is an anti-pattern), secrets in ENV or ARG (use --mount=type=secret), secrets in COPY (credentials, private keys, .env files), privileged operations in final stage, SETUID/SETGID binaries in final image, writable sensitive directories. Verify: read-only root filesystem compatible (no writes to / at runtime), no unnecessary capabilities (drop ALL, add only needed), signal handling correct (PID 1 with tini or --init). Scan final image with trivy/grype: zero critical/high CVEs. Verify no shell in distroless/scratch images (reduces attack surface). Verify COPY --chmod for restrictive file permissions.
verifies: non-root USER in final stage (numeric UID)
no secrets in ENV, ARG, or COPY
no SETUID/SETGID binaries in final image

delegates.task_template: Audit container supply chain security for: ${goal}. Verify: base images pinned by digest (sha256) or exact version tag, image provenance verified (Docker Content Trust or cosign). Verify: SBOM generated for final image (syft or trivy), vulnerability scanning in CI pipeline, image signing configured (cosign or Notary). Audit: no curl | sh patterns in RUN (download, verify checksum, then execute), all downloaded binaries verified (sha256sum/gpg). Verify .dockerignore excludes secrets. Flag images from untrusted registries. Verify no packages installed from untrusted sources (no custom apt repos without GPG verification). Classify threat level using S0-S3 severity. Verify image scanning runs before push to registry.
verifies: base images pinned by digest or exact version
no untrusted registries or apt repos

phases.checkpoint: Non-root runtime, zero CVEs, supply chain signed and verified
phases.failure_message: Safety gate failed. Root user, exposed secrets, CVEs, or unsigned images detected.

phases.name: Testing & Coverage
phases.principle: Is every code path tested with evidence?

delegates.task_template: Enforce comprehensive Dockerfile testing for: ${goal}. Test with container-structure-test (Google): verify file existence, command output, metadata (exposed ports, entrypoint, user, workdir, env vars, labels). Test with dgoss/goss: verify running container behavior (ports listening, processes running, file permissions, package versions). Test HEALTHCHECK: verify health endpoint responds within timeout. Test multi-stage: verify build stage produces expected artifacts, verify final stage excludes build tools. Test image startup: verify container starts within 5s, verify graceful shutdown (SIGTERM handled within 10s). Test with docker run --read-only: verify application works with read-only root. Test environment variable configuration: required vars fail fast with clear error, optional vars use correct defaults. Verify .dockerignore effectiveness: build with --no-cache and verify context size.
verifies: build stage excluded from final image verified
required env vars fail fast with clear error

phases.checkpoint: Structure tests pass, runtime behavior verified, startup/shutdown tested, read-only compatible
phases.failure_message: Testing gate failed. Missing structure tests, slow startup, or broken health checks detected.

phases.name: CI & Automation
phases.principle: Does CI enforce every standard automatically?

delegates.task_template: Enforce full Dockerfile CI pipeline for: ${goal}. Hadolint on ALL Dockerfiles: treat all warnings as errors (hadolint --failure-threshold warning), enforce DL3006 (pin versions), DL3007 (no latest tag), DL3008 (pin apt versions), DL3015 (no-install-recommends), DL3018 (pin apk versions), DL3025 (JSON CMD), DL3002 (non-root). Trivy/grype image scan: fail on critical/high. Container-structure-test in CI. Docker buildx build with --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64. Image size check with threshold. Pre-commit hooks: hadolint, dockerfile-utils lint. CI caches BuildKit layers. Verify image push only from CI (not local). SLO enforcement: build <120s, scan <30s. No '|| true' on any step. Nightly job for base image CVE monitoring.
verifies: trivy/grype scan in CI (fail on critical/high)
nightly CVE monitoring for base images

phases.checkpoint: CI enforces hadolint, scanning, structure tests, multi-arch builds
phases.failure_message: CI gate failed. Hadolint not enforced, missing scans, or incomplete build matrix.

phases.name: Documentation
phases.principle: Is every public API documented with runnable examples?

delegates.task_template: Audit and perfect all Dockerfile documentation for: ${goal}. Every Dockerfile MUST have header comments: purpose, base image rationale, build instructions, runtime configuration. Document: all ARG variables with descriptions and defaults, all ENV variables with purpose and examples, exposed ports with protocol and service, HEALTHCHECK endpoint and expected response, volume mount points with data descriptions. README MUST include: docker build command with all required --build-arg, docker run command with all required -e vars and -v mounts, docker-compose example (if applicable), environment variable reference table, port mapping table, health check verification command, troubleshooting section (common build/run errors). Document image tagging strategy and registry location.
verifies: all ARG/ENV variables documented with defaults
README with build, run, and compose examples

delegates.task_template: Audit all Dockerfile documentation and user-facing content for brand voice compliance for: ${goal}. Enforce the Euxis Standard: authoritative, concise, imperative mood. Cut all hedging ('you might', 'it seems', 'this should'), filler ('basically', 'simply', 'just'), passive voice, and corporate jargon ('leverage', 'utilize', 'robust', 'facilitate'). Verify: short declarative sentences (8-15 words), benefit before mechanism, active voice always, one idea per sentence. Audit Dockerfile comments, README, docker-compose.yml comments, and error messages in entrypoint scripts. Output word count reduction and principle violations found.
verifies: brand voice audit complete with metrics

phases.checkpoint: Dockerfiles documented, env vars referenced, build/run examples complete, brand voice enforced
phases.failure_message: Documentation gate failed. Missing Dockerfile comments, undocumented env vars, or voice violations detected.

phases.name: API Design & UX
phases.principle: Is the API ergonomic, discoverable, and user-friendly?

delegates.task_template: Audit Dockerfile and container UX for: ${goal}. Verify entrypoint script UX: required env vars fail fast with actionable error message listing missing vars, --help flag shows configuration reference, log output uses structured format (JSON) with configurable log level. Verify container lifecycle: starts within 5s, responds to SIGTERM gracefully (connection draining), HEALTHCHECK provides meaningful status. Verify configuration ergonomics: sensible defaults for all env vars, .env.example documents all options, docker-compose.yml ready to use with one command (docker compose up). Verify observability: logs to stdout/stderr (not files), metrics endpoint if applicable, container labels for discovery. Verify developer experience: docker compose up starts full local environment, hot-reload supported in dev mode, clear separation between dev and prod Dockerfiles/targets.
verifies: required env vars fail fast with actionable error
docker compose up works with one command
dev vs prod targets clearly separated

phases.checkpoint: Container UX polished, fail-fast config, graceful lifecycle, compose-ready
phases.failure_message: API design gate failed. Missing fail-fast errors, poor lifecycle handling, or broken compose setup.

phases.name: Hardening & Release
phases.principle: Is it release-ready with baselines, changelogs, and semver?

delegates.task_template: Stress test and establish image baselines for: ${goal}. Measure: final image size (compressed and uncompressed), layer count and individual layer sizes (dive), build time with cold cache and warm cache, container startup time to healthy, memory usage at idle and under load, container shutdown time on SIGTERM. Set budgets: image size (max for language), startup <5s, shutdown <10s, idle memory <50MB (adjust per workload). Test under load: concurrent requests, resource limits (--memory=256m, --cpus=0.5), OOM behavior (graceful or crash). Verify no zombie processes under load. Verify log volume under load (no excessive logging). Target: smallest possible image, fastest startup, graceful degradation under resource pressure, low memory, low CPU.

Perform exhaustive regression analysis for: ${goal}. Verify CI catches: image size regressions (threshold check), CVE regressions (new vulnerabilities in base or deps), build time regressions, startup time regressions, hadolint regressions (new warnings). Classify every change by risk: base image updates, dependency version changes, layer reordering, user/permission changes, port changes, env var changes (breaking for consumers). Scan for deprecated Dockerfile instructions or base images approaching EOL. Verify no test/dev artifacts leaked to production image. Check docker-compose.yml version compatibility. Verify CI blocks regression merges.
verifies: no deprecated instructions or EOL base images
no test/dev artifacts in production image

delegates.task_template: Verify release readiness for: ${goal}. Execute pre-release checklist: hadolint clean, trivy/grype zero critical/high, structure tests pass, multi-arch builds succeed, image size within budget, documentation current. Determine correct semantic version bump: MAJOR for breaking env var/port/volume changes, MINOR for new features/config options, PATCH for security patches/bug fixes. Verify image tagged correctly (semver + git SHA + latest). Generate changelog following Keep a Changelog format. Verify upgrade/migration instructions: volume data compatibility, env var renames, port changes. Draft release notes with image size and security scan results. Verify rollback plan (previous image tag accessible). Coordinate security clearance with sentinel.
verifies: release notes with image size and scan results

phases.checkpoint: Image baselined, regressions blocked, release-ready with tags and changelog
phases.failure_message: Hardening findings detected. Review image baselines, regressions, and release readiness.

phases.name: Final Truth Gate
phases.principle: Is every claim backed by evidence?

delegates.task_template: Final gate for: ${goal}. Validate evidence from all prior phases. Verify: architecture scores at target (Tech Debt 10/10, Modularity 100/100), multi-stage builds enforced, base images pinned, .dockerignore comprehensive, modern idioms throughout, best practices researched with citations, hadolint zero warnings, non-root runtime, zero critical/high CVEs, no secrets in image, supply chain signed, structure tests and runtime tests pass, multi-arch (amd64 + arm64), image size within budget, startup <5s, graceful shutdown, all env vars documented, brand voice audit passed, CI enforces all gates, regressions blocked, release readiness confirmed. Cross-check agent findings for contradictions. Issue PASS or FAIL.
verifies: all gate claims backed by evidence

delegates.task_template: Compliance audit for: ${goal}. Scan for PII exposure in container logs, environment variables, and image layers (docker history --no-trunc). Verify no secrets baked into any image layer (even intermediate layers accessible via docker save). Audit base image licenses: verify compatibility with project license. Verify SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) generated and accurate. Check for unauthorized network access from container (egress rules documented). Verify image registry access controls (no public push of private images). Verify OCI labels include required metadata (maintainer, version, description, source URL). Flag any compliance violation with regulatory reference and remediation. Issue PASS, FAIL, or WARN verdict.
verifies: no PII in logs or env vars
no secrets in any image layer (docker history)

delegates.task_template: Adversarial review of all gate findings for: ${goal}. Challenge assumptions from every prior phase. For each gate's PASS claim: invert the assumption and test what happens if wrong. Challenge security claims: does non-root user truly prevent privilege escalation, or are there SETUID binaries, writable /proc, or kernel exploits in the base image? Challenge image size claims: is the image minimal, or are there hidden layers with build artifacts accessible via docker save and layer extraction? Challenge multi-arch claims: does building for arm64 mean it actually runs correctly, or are there architecture-dependent runtime behaviors untested? Perform pre-mortem: assume the container caused a security breach 6 months later, enumerate top 5 reasons. Assess blast radius (lateral movement from compromised container). If all agents agree on PASS, verify it is not groupthink. Issue PROCEED, PROCEED WITH SAFEGUARDS, or RECONSIDER verdict.
verifies: image minimality challenged (hidden layers, docker save)
multi-arch challenged (runtime behavior vs build success)

phases.checkpoint: Reviewer sign-off, compliance cleared, critic challenge passed
phases.failure_message: Final truth gate failed. Unverified claims, compliance issues, or critic objections detected.
